home.visitor.title: Login to View Personal Admissions Data
home.visitor.desc: Rankings and basic admission data are open to everyone. Login to unlock: GPA matching analysis, IELTS score filtering, personal strategy, three-phase planning and more.
home.highlights.title: Today's Highlights
highlights.1.title: Main vs Satellite Campuses
highlights.1.desc: UTM/UTSC and UBC Okanagan admission scores 10-15% lower — a great pathway into top universities
highlights.2.title: Math ~9pts easier than CS
highlights.2.desc: Math admission typically 9-10pts lower than CS at same university, e.g. McMaster Math 85% vs CS 94%
highlights.3.title: Tuition gap up to ¥1M
highlights.3.desc: Memorial ¥60-90K/yr vs UTSG ¥210-340K/yr — 4-year difference up to ¥1M
highlights.4.title: 18+ universities offer dual admission
highlights.4.desc: Dual enrollment at 18/26 schools, minimum IELTS 4.0 accepted for pathway programs
highlights.5.title: 20+ universities have Co-op
highlights.5.desc: Most offer Co-op in Engineering/CS — gain work experience while earning tuition
highlights.6.title: Australia Foundation from IELTS 5.0
highlights.6.desc: UNSW & QS Top 50 foundation programs accept IELTS 5.0, 91% progression rate
highlights.7.title: Satellite campus 'score discount'
highlights.7.desc: UTM CS mid-high 80s vs UTSG ~93% — same degree, 10-15% easier entry
highlights.8.title: 15pt difference within same school
highlights.8.desc: Psychology 10-15pts lower than CS at same school — choosing the right major matters
highlights.9.title: Ranking ≠ Admission Difficulty
highlights.9.desc: Some QS Top 100 universities admit at 75-80% for certain programs
highlights.10.title: Early application advantage
highlights.10.desc: Popular programs close Jan 15; early rolling admission locks in seats
highlights.11.title: 75%+ of resources in ON/BC
highlights.11.desc: Toronto/Vancouver offer most internships and jobs among tracked universities
highlights.12.title: 'Niche' programs at elite schools
highlights.12.desc: UofT/McMaster admit at 80-85% for some programs vs 95%+ for CS/Engineering

files.subtitle: Reports · Policy Documents · Study Abroad Resources
files.doc1.title: 14th Five-Year Plan for Education Development
files.doc1.desc: Top-level design for building an education powerhouse by 2030. 5 strategic tasks + 3 major plans + 6 comprehensive reforms. Covers AI education, Double First-Class expansion, overseas study service system, education evaluation reform.
files.doc2.desc: Evaluates 89 global economies on higher education-to-workforce match. 4 dimensions: Skills Match, Academic Readiness, Future of Work, Economic Transformation. Canada ranked 5th overall.
files.more: More reports coming soon · Stay tuned
